summary: Matched the first four Sysdolphin RObj functions by introducing src/sysdolphin/robj.c and robj.h, moving HSD_RObjInitAllocData, HSD_RObjGetAllocData, HSD_RvalueObjGetAllocData, and func_8037AE90 out of asm/robj.s. The PR also renamed func_8037AE90 references in assembly callsites to HSD_RObjSetFlags, added RObj/Rvalue/IK/Exp-related struct definitions, tightened TEV alloc-data types from void*/u8 arrays to HSD_ObjAllocData*, and removed now-unneeded force_active/peephole pragma scaffolding in AObj, ID, and initialize code.
what_changed: Added src/sysdolphin/robj.c containing C implementations for HSD_RObjInitAllocData, HSD_RObjGetAllocData, HSD_RvalueObjGetAllocData, and HSD_RObjSetFlags.
Removed the corresponding assembly bodies from asm/robj.s, leaving later RObj functions still in assembly.
Added src/sysdolphin/robj.h with RObj-related constants, structs, and function prototypes.
Added src/sysdolphin/robj.o to TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk before asm/robj.o so the newly matched C functions are linked.
Updated existing assembly callsites in asm/code_8000B1CC.s from func_8037AE90 to the newly named HSD_RObjSetFlags.

Changed TEV alloc-data externs from raw u8 arrays to HSD_ObjAllocData objects and changed HSD_RenderGetAllocData, HSD_TevRegGetAllocData, and HSD_ChanGetAllocData return types from void* to HSD_ObjAllocData*.
Included objalloc.h from tev.h so TEV alloc-data accessors can expose HSD_ObjAllocData* in their public prototypes.
Removed a force_active pragma block around _HSD_AObjForgetMemory.
Collapsed or removed peephole pragma push/pop scaffolding around _HSD_IDForgetMemory and HSD_GXSetFifoObj.
smart_moves: Started RObj decompilation with small leaf/simple allocator functions, which reduced asm while minimizing risk.
Named func_8037AE90 as HSD_RObjSetFlags based on its behavior: NULL check followed by OR-ing the supplied flags into robj->flags.
Used real allocator storage types for TEV globals instead of byte arrays plus casts, making later code and prototypes safer and clearer.
Introduced robj.h at the same time as robj.c, capturing the struct layout knowledge needed for the matched functions and for later RObj work.
Kept existing unknown static/global labels such as lbl_804C2368 and lbl_804C2394 as externs with descriptive comments, avoiding unsupported global renames while documenting intended meaning.
Updated assembly symbol references to the new C function name, improving cross-file readability even where callers were still in asm.
Removed decompilation-only pragma scaffolding once it was no longer needed for matching, reducing fragile compiler-control clutter.
decomp_lessons: Allocator init/getter functions are good early targets: matching can often be achieved by declaring the backing HSD_ObjAllocData globals and calling HSD_ObjAllocInit with sizeof(struct) and alignment parameters.
When a function's behavior is obvious from assembly, a semantic name like HSD_RObjSetFlags can be introduced and propagated to assembly callsites before all callers are decompiled.
Replacing raw byte-array externs with the true struct type can eliminate casts and improve function signatures without changing behavior.
Header work is part of matching: struct definitions, unions, constants, and prototypes may be needed before even small C functions can be expressed cleanly.
Pragma push/pop or force_active blocks should be revisited after nearby functions match; some were only temporary matching aids.
Partial module migration is viable: add src/sysdolphin/robj.o before asm/robj.o and delete only the matched assembly ranges, while leaving the rest of asm/robj.s intact.
naming_conventions: Sysdolphin public functions use HSD_ prefixes, e.g. HSD_RObjInitAllocData, HSD_RObjGetAllocData, HSD_RvalueObjGetAllocData, HSD_RObjSetFlags.
RObj type names follow HSD_RObj, HSD_RObjDesc, HSD_RObjAnimJoint, with struct tags using underscored forms like struct _HSD_RObj.
Rvalue names in this PR use HSD_Rvalue and HSD_RvalueList, with the accessor spelled HSD_RvalueObjGetAllocData.
RObj constants were introduced as uppercase macros: TYPE_MASK, REFTYPE_JOBJ, REFTYPE_IKHINT.
Unknown data labels remained as lbl_804C2368/lbl_804C2394 with comments documenting likely names such as robj_alloc_data and rvalue_alloc_data.
TEV allocator getters use HSD_RenderGetAllocData, HSD_TevRegGetAllocData, and HSD_ChanGetAllocData and now return HSD_ObjAllocData*.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: HSD_RObjSetFlags matches a simple pattern: if the HSD_RObj pointer is non-NULL, OR flags into offset 0x04 and return.

HSD_RObjInitAllocData matches by calling HSD_ObjAllocInit twice: once for RObj allocation data with sizeof(HSD_RObj) and once for Rvalue allocation data with sizeof(HSD_Rvalue), both with alignment/count argument 4.
The deleted asm shows HSD_RObjGetAllocData and HSD_RvalueObjGetAllocData were simple address-returning functions, making them straightforward C matches.
The build was adjusted by inserting src/sysdolphin/robj.o before asm/robj.o, enabling mixed C/asm ownership of the RObj text section.
The PR used symbol renaming in existing assembly callsites rather than waiting for those callers to be decompiled.
TEV cleanup removed casts in HSD_ObjAllocInit calls by typing globals as HSD_ObjAllocData.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that continue matching remaining functions in asm/robj.s after func_8037AEA8.
Search for later renames of lbl_804C2368 and lbl_804C2394 to robj_alloc_data and rvalue_alloc_data.
Check whether TYPE_MASK, REFTYPE_JOBJ, and REFTYPE_IKHINT were later renamed or expanded with additional RObj flag constants.
Find follow-up work that replaces raw TEV allocation sizes 0x1C, 0x14, and 0x30 with sizeof concrete structs.
Search for later removal of NON_MATCHING around HSD_RenderInitAllocData.
